canonical_representation: 741-bit integer over lexicographically ordered unordered node pairs
duplicate_removal: Python integer set keyed by the canonical adjacency bitset
enumeration_algorithm: Breadth-first traversal from the observed graph over every valid labelled simple-graph 2-switch
completeness_basis: Any two labelled simple graphs with the same degree sequence are connected by a finite sequence of 2-switches: their symmetric difference decomposes into alternating even circuits, each removable by successive 2-switches. Therefore the exhaustive BFS component from the observed realization is the full realization set; no additional components exist.
ensemble_conclusion: All 63 labelled simple graphs satisfying the observed degree sequence; one connected switch-state graph; exact equal weighting is a complete fixed-degree enumeration.
